---
title: "Knowledge Graph — Obsidian Plugin Demo"
subtitle: "AI-Powered Study Item Generation & Concept Management"
author: "DSAN 6725 Team"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    code-fold: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    highlight-style: github-dark
    transition: slide
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    footer: "Knowledge Graph Plugin v0.1.0"
---

## What We Built {.center}

An **Obsidian plugin** backed by a **FastAPI server** that turns your
personal notes into an AI-driven study system.

::: {.incremental}
- Write concepts as markdown notes in your vault
- The plugin reads your knowledge graph (concepts + edges)
- An LLM pipeline generates study items tailored to **your** knowledge
- You answer, get graded, and progress through difficulty tiers
:::

---

## Architecture Overview {.smaller}

Two feedback cycles drive every round. **Solid** = data flow, **dashed** = feedback edge.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 12
%%| fig-height: 4.5
flowchart LR
    %% --- Sample item evaluation (pre-round) ---
    Sample["Sample item<br/>(md / image)"] ==> SE["<b>Sample Evaluator</b><br/>multimodal pre-grading"]
    SE -. "analysis_notes" .-> Gen

    %% --- Core hardening loop (during round) ---
    Gen["<b>Generator</b>"] ==> Act["<b>Actor ×3</b><br/>simulated solutions"]
    Act ==> Ref["<b>Reflector</b><br/>quality + hardening"]
    Ref -. "hardening directives<br/>(if difficulty ≥ hard)" .-> Gen
    Ref ==> Items["Items in vault"]

    classDef phase fill:#1e293b,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#e2e8f0,stroke-width:2px;
    classDef data  fill:#0f172a,stroke:#475569,color:#cbd5e1,stroke-width:1px;
    class SE,Gen,Act,Ref phase
    class Sample,Items data
```

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
*Per-concept Answer Evaluator (post-reply → Elo mastery + `focus_depth`) is detailed two slides on.*
:::

---

## The Generation Pipeline {.smaller}

A round chains a **pre-grading pass + 5-stage loop + per-concept answer grading**, all via LLM tool-calling:

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| **Sample Evaluator** *(pre-round, multimodal)* | Pre-grades sample reference (md / image) → `analysis_notes` for Generator |
| **Feasibility** | Gates whether requested concepts × difficulty is generatable |
| **Generator** | Produces N items from concepts, edges, examples, images, `analysis_notes` |
| **Actor** ×3 | Simulated students solve each item in parallel |
| **Reflector** | Scores quality, drives refinement (+hardening for hard+) |
| **Round Evaluator** | Summarizes round quality, supports difficulty progression |
| **Answer Evaluator** *(per-concept)* | Verdicts `correct / alt_path / misunderstood / untested` → Elo mastery |

Structured tool-calling (Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5) — no free-text concatenation.

---

## Iterative Hardening (hard / very_hard / expert)

For difficulty ≥ **hard**, items go through up to 3 hardening iterations:

::: {.incremental}
1. Generate items at **medium** difficulty first
2. **Actor** solves them — produces reasoning trajectories
3. **Hardening Reflector** analyzes trajectories:
   - "Actor solved trivially → add multi-step reasoning"
   - "High confidence → inject edge cases"
   - "Few concepts used → require cross-concept synthesis"
4. Reflector also pulls **random extra concepts** from your graph
5. **Generator** re-creates items with hardening directives
6. Repeat — `item(i+1) = f(item(i))`, single-slot context (no accumulation)
:::

---

## Five Difficulty Tiers

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 8
flowchart LR
    E["Easy"] --> M["Medium"] --> H["Hard\n(+hardening)"]
    H --> VH["Very Hard\n(+hardening)"]
    VH --> EX["Expert\n(+hardening)"]

    style E fill:#4CAF50,color:#fff
    style M fill:#FFC107,color:#000
    style H fill:#FF9800,color:#fff
    style VH fill:#F44336,color:#fff
    style EX fill:#9C27B0,color:#fff
```

- User **manually selects** difficulty — no forced auto-escalation
- Hard+ triggers the iterative hardening loop above
- Extra concepts from your knowledge graph make items harder but solvable

---

## Plugin UI — Concept Import

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Adding Concepts

- **Search bar** — fuzzy search vault notes
- **Add Active Note** — one-click current note
- **Import Active + Linked** — pulls wikilinked notes
- **Import All Vault** — bulk load

Each note's YAML frontmatter provides:

```yaml
---
mastery: 7
edges:
  - target: "Conditional Probability"
    type: prerequisite
    direction: directed
---
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Managing Edges

- Auto-populated from frontmatter
- Manual add with type dropdown:
  - prerequisite, derivation, application
  - reference, contrast, analogy, custom
- Direction: directed / undirected
:::

::::

---

## Plugin UI — Context Images & PDF Upload

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Inline Context Images

Attach exam paper images as visual context:

- **Attach Image** — file picker (png, jpg, webp)
- **From Vault** — pick images in your vault

Images are base64-encoded and sent **inline** with
the tool-calling JSON — Claude sees the image alongside
concept context when generating items.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### PDF → Concept Graph

Upload a PDF paper:

1. **MinerU** extracts text to markdown
2. **LLM** segments into concept nodes + directed edges
3. Notes auto-created in vault with YAML frontmatter
4. Concepts + edges auto-populated in the generator form

Instantly turns any paper into a study-ready knowledge graph.
:::

::::

---

## Plugin UI — Session Flow

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 10
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant P as Plugin
    participant B as Backend
    participant L as LLM Pipeline

    U->>P: Select concepts, edges, difficulty
    U->>P: Click "Generate Items"
    P->>B: POST /api/v1/items/generate
    B->>L: Generator → Actors → Reflector → Round Evaluator
    L-->>B: RoundResult (items + solutions)
    B-->>P: JSON response
    P->>P: Create Items/session-*.md

    Note over U: User writes answers in callouts

    U->>P: Click "Grade My Answers"
    P->>B: POST /api/v1/items/grade-answer
    B-->>P: Feedback per answer
    P->>P: Inject feedback into note

    U->>P: Select difficulty, click "Next Round"
    P->>B: POST /api/v1/items/continue-round
    B->>L: (hardening if hard+)
    L-->>B: New items
    B-->>P: Append to session note
```

---

## Session Resume & Sample Items

### Resume
- Session state lives in the **markdown note** (YAML frontmatter)
- Pick any `Items/*.md` from the dropdown → resume generating rounds
- Survives Obsidian restarts, backend restarts — fully stateless

### Sample Items
- `SampleItems/` folder in your vault
- Paste exam problems as reference items
- Plugin reads them and feeds to generator as `example_items`
- Generator mimics the style/format of your samples

---

## Answer Grading

The plugin reads your answers from markdown callouts and grades them:

````markdown
#### Your Answer

> [!quote]- Write your answer here
> P(A|B) = P(B|A) * P(A) / P(B)
> Using the law of total probability for the denominator...

#### Evaluator Feedback  ← injected by plugin
> [!success] Score: 8.5/10
> Strong application of Bayes' theorem.
> Consider explicitly stating the partition used in the denominator.
````

- LLM compares your answer against the reference solution
- Feedback injected directly into the note
- Scores visible before choosing next round difficulty

---

## Cancel Generation

Misclicks happen — the "Next Round" button has a safety toggle:

::: {.incremental}
1. Click **"Next Round"** → button becomes **"Cancel Generation"**
2. Click again → generation result is **discarded** client-side
3. UI resets — no phantom items appended to your session note
:::

The backend request completes server-side (Obsidian API limitation),
but the plugin ignores the response.

---

## Setup for Your Team {.smaller}

### 1. Clone & install backend

```bash
git clone https://github.com/chaowei312/finalproj-backend.git
cd finalproj-backend
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### 2. Configure `.env` (copy from `.env.example`)

```bash
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
ITEM_GENERATION_PROVIDER=anthropic
```

### 3. Start the server

```bash
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
```

### 4. Build & install the plugin

```bash
cd plugin
npm install && npm run build
```

Copy `plugin/main.js`, `plugin/manifest.json`, `plugin/styles.css`
into your vault at `.obsidian/plugins/knowledge-graph/`.

Enable the plugin in Obsidian Settings → Community Plugins.

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| **Frontend** | Obsidian plugin (TypeScript, esbuild) |
| **Backend** | FastAPI (Python 3.11+, async) |
| **LLM** | Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 (tool-calling) |
| **LLM Fallback** | Groq (OpenAI-compatible, free tier) |
| **PDF Extraction** | MinerU (`mineru[pipeline]`) |
| **Data Format** | Markdown + YAML frontmatter |
| **Session State** | Stateless — note IS the session |

---

## What's Next

::: {.incremental}
- **Concept CRUD** — full backend persistence (MongoDB)
- **Embedding pipeline** — semantic search via Pinecone
- **Graph visualization** — Cytoscape.js panel in Obsidian
- **Traversal engine** — KST outer-fringe personalized paths
- **Group landscapes** — merged canonical views across users
- **Standalone web UI** — move beyond Obsidian for broader access
:::

---

## Demo Time {.center}

Let's generate some study items live.

:::{.callout-tip}
### Try it yourself
1. Open Obsidian with the plugin installed
2. Click the brain icon in the left ribbon
3. Import your concept notes
4. Hit **Generate Items**
:::
